name: ovos-utterance-normalizer

metadata.name: ovos-utterance-normalizer
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License

documentation:
# ovos-utterance-normalizer

normalizes utterances before intent parsing

additionally, encoding errors are handled via [python-ftfy](https://github.com/rspeer/python-ftfy)

enabled by default in [mycroft.conf](https://github.com/OpenVoiceOS/ovos-config/blob/dev/ovos_config/mycroft.conf#L129)

modules.name: ovos_utterance_normalizer.version
documentation: START_VERSION_BLOCK

classes.name: ovos_utterance_normalizer.UtteranceNormalizerPlugin
documentation:
plugin to normalize utterances by normalizing numbers, punctuation and contractions
language specific pre-processing is handled here too
this helps intent parsers

functions.name: ovos_utterance_normalizer.tokenization.partition_list
documentation:
Partition a list of items.

Works similarly to str.partition

Args:
    items:
    split_on callable:
        Should return a boolean. Each item will be passed to
        this callable in succession, and partitions will be
        created any time it returns True.

Returns:
    [[any]]

classes.name: ovos_utterance_normalizer.tokenization.ReplaceableEntity
documentation:
Similar to Token, this class is used in entity parsing.

Once we've found an entity in a string, this class contains all
the info about the value, and where it came from in the original text.
In other words, it is the text, and the entity that can replace it in
the string.

classes.name: ovos_utterance_normalizer.tokenization.ReplaceableNumber
documentation:
Similar to Token, this class is used in number parsing.

Once we've found a number in a string, this class contains all
the info about the value, and where it came from in the original text.
In other words, it is the text, and the number that can replace it in
the string.

classes.name: ovos_utterance_normalizer.tokenization.ReplaceableDate
documentation:
Similar to Token, this class is used in date parsing.

Once we've found a date in a string, this class contains all
the info about the value, and where it came from in the original text.
In other words, it is the text, and the date that can replace it in
the string.

classes.name: ovos_utterance_normalizer.tokenization.ReplaceableTime
documentation:
Similar to Token, this class is used in date parsing.

Once we've found a time in a string, this class contains all
the info about the value, and where it came from in the original text.
In other words, it is the text, and the time that can replace it in
the string.

classes.name: ovos_utterance_normalizer.tokenization.ReplaceableTimedelta
documentation:
Similar to Token, this class is used in date parsing.

Once we've found a timedelta in a string, this class contains all
the info about the value, and where it came from in the original text.
In other words, it is the text, and the duration that can replace it in
the string.

classes.name: ovos_utterance_normalizer.normalizer.Normalizer

methods.name: ovos_utterance_normalizer.normalizer.Normalizer.expand_contractions
documentation: Expand common contractions, e.g. "isn't" -> "is not"

individual languages may subclass this if needed

normalize_XX should pass a valid config read from json

functions.name: ovos_utterance_normalizer.numeric.is_numeric
documentation:
Takes in a string and tests to see if it is a number.
Args:
    word (str): string to test if a number
Returns:
    (bool): True if a number, else False

functions.name: ovos_utterance_normalizer.numeric.look_for_fractions
documentation:
"
This function takes a list made by fraction & determines if a fraction.

Args:
    split_list (list): list created by splitting on '/'
Returns:
    (bool): False if not a fraction, otherwise True

methods.name: ovos_utterance_normalizer.numeric.GermanNumberParser.is_ordinal_de
documentation:
This function takes the given text and checks if it is an ordinal number.
Args:
    input_str (str): the string to check if ordinal
Returns:
    (bool) or (float): False if not an ordinal, otherwise the number
    corresponding to the ordinal
ordinals for 1, 3, 7 and 8 are irregular
only works for ordinals corresponding to the numbers in _STRING_NUM

methods.name: ovos_utterance_normalizer.numeric.GermanNumberParser.is_fractional_de
documentation:
This function takes the given text and checks if it is a fraction.
Args:
    input_str (str): the string to check if fractional
    short_scale (bool): use short scale if True, long scale if False
Returns:
    (bool) or (float): False if not a fraction, otherwise the fraction

methods.name: ovos_utterance_normalizer.numeric.GermanNumberParser.convert_words_to_numbers
documentation:
Convert words in a string into their equivalent numbers.
Args:
    text str:
    short_scale boolean: True if short scale numberres should be used.
    ordinals boolean: True if ordinals (e.g. first, second, third) should
                    be parsed to their number values (1, 2, 3...)
Returns:
    str
    The original text, with numbers subbed in where appropriate.

methods.name: ovos_utterance_normalizer.numeric.GermanNumberParser.extract_numbers
documentation:
extract numeric values from a list of tokens.
Args:
    tokens (list): list of tokens (str)
    short_scale boolean: True if short scale numbers should be used.
    ordinals boolean: True if ordinals (e.g. first, second, third) should
                      be parsed to their number values (1, 2, 3...)
Returns:
    list of extraced numbers (ReplaceableNumber)

methods.name: ovos_utterance_normalizer.numeric.GermanNumberParser._extract_numbers_with_text_de
documentation:
Extract all numbers from a list of Tokens, with the words that
represent them.

Args:
    [Token]: The tokens to parse.
    short_scale bool: True if short scale numbers should be used, False for
                    long scale. True by default.
    ordinals bool: True if ordinal words (first, second, third, etc) should
                be parsed.
    fractional_numbers bool: True if we should look for fractions and
                            decimals.

Returns:
    [ReplaceableNumber]: A list of tuples, each containing a number and a
                    string.

methods.name: ovos_utterance_normalizer.numeric.GermanNumberParser._extract_number_with_text_de
documentation:
This function extracts a number from a list of Tokens.

Args:
    tokens str: the string to normalize
    short_scale (bool): use short scale if True, long scale if False
    ordinals (bool): consider ordinal numbers
    fractional_numbers (bool): True if we should look for fractions and
                            decimals.
Returns:
    ReplaceableNumber

methods.name: ovos_utterance_normalizer.numeric.GermanNumberParser._extract_number_with_text_de_helper
documentation:
Helper for _extract_number_with_text_de.

Args:
    tokens [Token]:
    short_scale boolean:
    ordinals boolean:
    fractional_numbers boolean:
Returns:
    int or float, [Tokens]

methods.name: ovos_utterance_normalizer.numeric.GermanNumberParser._extract_real_number_with_text_de
documentation:
This is handling real numbers.

Args:
    tokens [Token]:
    short_scale boolean:
Returns:
    int or float, [Tokens]
    The value parsed, and tokens that it corresponds to.

classes.name: ovos_utterance_normalizer.numeric.EnglishNumberParser

methods.name: ovos_utterance_normalizer.numeric.EnglishNumberParser.is_fractional
documentation:
This function takes the given text and checks if it is a fraction.

Args:
    input_str (str): the string to check if fractional
    short_scale (bool): use short scale if True, long scale if False
    spoken (bool): consider "half", "quarter", "whole" a fraction
Returns:
    (bool) or (float): False if not a fraction, otherwise the fraction

methods.name: ovos_utterance_normalizer.numeric.EnglishNumberParser.convert_words_to_numbers
documentation:
Convert words in a string into their equivalent numbers.
Args:
    text str:
    short_scale boolean: True if short scale numbers should be used.
    ordinals boolean: True if ordinals (e.g. first, second, third) should
                      be parsed to their number values (1, 2, 3...)

Returns:
    str
    The original text, with numbers subbed in where appropriate.

methods.name: ovos_utterance_normalizer.numeric.EnglishNumberParser.extract_numbers
documentation:
extract numeric values from a list of tokens.
Args:
    tokens (list): list of tokens (str)
    short_scale boolean: True if short scale numbers should be used.
    ordinals boolean: True if ordinals (e.g. first, second, third) should
                      be parsed to their number values (1, 2, 3...)
Returns:
    list of extraced numbers (ReplaceableNumber)

methods.name: ovos_utterance_normalizer.numeric.EnglishNumberParser._initialize_number_data_en
documentation:
Generate dictionaries of words to numbers, based on scale.

This is a helper function for _extract_whole_number.

Args:
    short_scale (bool):
    speech (bool): consider extra words (_SPOKEN_EXTRA_NUM_EN) to be numbers

Returns:
    (set(str), dict(str, number), dict(str, number))
    multiplies, string_num_ordinal, string_num_scale
helper methods

methods.name: ovos_utterance_normalizer.numeric.EnglishNumberParser._extract_fraction_with_text_en
documentation:
Extract fraction numbers from a string.

This function handles text such as '2 and 3/4'. Note that "one half" or
similar will be parsed by the whole number function.

Args:
    tokens [Token]: words and their indexes in the original string.
    short_scale boolean:
    ordinals boolean:

Returns:
    (int or float, [Token])
    The value found, and the list of relevant tokens.
    (None, None) if no fraction value is found.

methods.name: ovos_utterance_normalizer.numeric.EnglishNumberParser._extract_decimal_with_text_en
documentation:
Extract decimal numbers from a string.

This function handles text such as '2 point 5'.

Notes:
    While this is a helper for extractnumber_en, it also depends on
    extractnumber_en, to parse out the components of the decimal.

    This does not currently handle things like:
        number dot number number number

Args:
    tokens [Token]: The text to parse.
    short_scale boolean:
    ordinals boolean:

Returns:
    (float, [Token])
    The value found and relevant tokens.
    (None, None) if no decimal value is found.

methods.name: ovos_utterance_normalizer.numeric.EnglishNumberParser._extract_whole_number_with_text_en
documentation:
Handle numbers not handled by the decimal or fraction functions. This is
generally whole numbers. Note that phrases such as "one half" will be
handled by this function, while "one and a half" are handled by the
fraction function.

Args:
    tokens [Token]:
    short_scale boolean:
    ordinals boolean:

Returns:
    int or float, [Tokens]
    The value parsed, and tokens that it corresponds to.

methods.name: ovos_utterance_normalizer.numeric.EnglishNumberParser._extract_number_with_text_en_helper
documentation:
Helper for _extract_number_with_text_en.

This contains the real logic for parsing, but produces
a result that needs a little cleaning (specific, it may
contain leading articles that can be trimmed off).

Args:
    tokens [Token]:
    short_scale boolean:
    ordinals boolean:
    fractional_numbers boolean:

Returns:
    int or float, [Tokens]

methods.name: ovos_utterance_normalizer.numeric.EnglishNumberParser._extract_number_with_text_en
documentation:
This function extracts a number from a list of Tokens.

Args:
    tokens str: the string to normalize
    short_scale (bool): use short scale if True, long scale if False
    ordinals (bool): consider ordinal numbers, third=3 instead of 1/3
    fractional_numbers (bool): True if we should look for fractions and
                               decimals.
Returns:
    ReplaceableNumber

methods.name: ovos_utterance_normalizer.numeric.EnglishNumberParser._extract_numbers_with_text_en
documentation:
Extract all numbers from a list of Tokens, with the words that
represent them.

Args:
    [Token]: The tokens to parse.
    short_scale bool: True if short scale numbers should be used, False for
                      long scale. True by default.
    ordinals bool: True if ordinal words (first, second, third, etc) should
                   be parsed.
    fractional_numbers bool: True if we should look for fractions and
                             decimals.

Returns:
    [ReplaceableNumber]: A list of tuples, each containing a number and a
                     string.
TODO - finish adding public user facing methods
